rationale: The citing paper references Hershfield et al. (2011) only once in the introduction as part of a grouped list of real-world intertemporal behaviors (e.g., mortgage choice, retirement savings, health management). The citation functions as broad background motivation for why intertemporal choice matters, not as a specific conceptual or empirical basis for the paper's hypotheses. The citing paper's theory and mechanism development centers on spatial time orientation, attention, and duration judgments, without distinct use of Hershfield et al.'s future-self intervention framework.
